title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Seagoing Laden Speed
Seagoing Laden Speed in the Suezmax and Aframax increased to the highest levels in
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform- Seagoing Laden Speed https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-vesselSpeeds/2p1zfx/325063059?h=_5duIxsKNwwK3MyEfMxUtikrRYIFdcdR8OhRb9bfaUE
June has witnessed a persistently weaker sentiment in crude freight rates. Nevertheless, a noteworthy development has been the notable increase in seagoing-laden speed. Despite the ongoing challenges in supply and demand dynamics, the first half of the year has witnessed an upward trend in the seagoing-laden speed of Suezmax and Aframax tankers.

Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T124651.881.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t124651881_20ee5217ffe7.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform- Seagoing Laden Speed https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-vesselSpeeds/2p1zfx/325063059?h=_5duIxsKNwwK3MyEfMxUtikrRYIFdcdR8OhRb9bfaUE
June has witnessed a persistently weaker sentiment in crude freight rates. Nevertheless, a noteworthy development has been the notable increase in seagoing-laden speed.

children.title: ‘Dirty' VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T124835.795.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t124835795_e91305ef9cc4.png
Early June continues to witness a prevailing weaker sentiment in the crude oil freight market, particularly in the Suezmax and Aframax segments, which are experiencing a notable downward trend. VLCC AG-FE freight rates are now around WS46, a 60% drop from the peak observed in week 12.

children.title: ‘Product' LR Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T125713.424.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t125713424_c06701d27e0e.png
During the first week of June, LR2 AG and LR1 MEG Japan freight rates experienced a notable decline, indicating a weaker outlook. Currently, both rates are approaching approximately 135 Worldscale (WS), which reflects a decrease of nearly 12 points compared to the rates observed just a week ago.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR1 Weaker | MR2 Softening
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T125804.693.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t125804693_8a5b51365002.png
The MR1 rates for shipments from Algeria to the Mediterranean have remained unchanged, settling at the weak levels observed in the previous week, approximately around WS135. Conversely, there has been a slight improvement in the MR1 rates for the Baltic Continent, which have now experienced a mild increase to levels around WS143.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - (# vessels) Decreasing 'Dirty' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Are as
section_path: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - (# vessels) Decreasing 'Dirty' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Are as
children.section_path_text: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - (# vessels) Decreasing 'Dirty' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Are as
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T125847.131.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t125847131_f1232a8e4ce2.png
The first week of June ends with a downward revision in the supply of VLCC and Suezmax crude oil tankers, while Aframax Primorsk continue to show signs of increasing. VLCC Ras Tanura: The current vessel count stands at 42, a deviation of 30 vessels from the annual average and a significant drop of almost 50% from the peak in week 18.

children.title: ‘Clean' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas |LR2| Decreasing
section_path: ‘Clean' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas |LR2| Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘Clean' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas |LR2| Decreasing

children.title: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
section_path: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T130000.696.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t130000696_727a1dd0993f.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The significant downward trend observed in May, which saw rates fall below the annual average, has persisted into June. Currently, the number of ships stands at 7, indicating a relatively low level of activity.

children.title: ‘Clean' | Panamax - MR Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-12T130113.083.png | assets/2023-06-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-12t130113083_644c5405cb3f.png
Dirty demand ton-days: Amid indications of a stronger demand outlook in the Suezmax segment, there is a growing sense of optimism in the VLCC market. In recent days, the VLCC segment has experienced higher growth rates compared to levels observed three weeks ago.
